text.title: One Linter+Formatter Per Language
text.summary: Each language gets exactly one linter+formatter — ruff for Python, biome for JS/TS — and the agent calls --fix/--write after every edit before running tests.

section.title: Single tool, deterministic invocation
section.p: Ruff and biome each subsume a chain of older tools (black + flake8 + isort + pyupgrade for Python; ESLint + Prettier for JS/TS). Running both the legacy chain and the new tool re-introduces the disagreement problem they exist to solve, and consumes the agent's tool-call budget on no-op fights.

rule.statement: For every Python source file, the agent MUST run `ruff check --fix` and then `ruff format` after each edit; for every JS/TS/JSX/TSX source file, the agent MUST run `biome check --write` after each edit. Repositories MUST NOT also configure black, flake8, isort, autoflake, pyupgrade, ESLint, or Prettier — these tools' configs (`.flake8`, `.eslintrc`, `.prettierrc`) MUST be removed when migrating.
rule.rationale: One canonical formatter per language gives the agent a fixed, sub-second autofix step; the formatter's output is the file's canonical form, so subsequent edits never trigger formatting churn. Both tools are single static Rust binaries with no plug-in resolution, which is why their cold-start beats the legacy chain by 10–100x and makes the inner loop reflexion-friendly.

example.code: # agent inner loop after editing src/users.py $ uv run ruff check --fix src/users.py $ uv run ruff format src/users.py $ uv run pytest tests/test_users.py
example.note: One linter, one formatter, one test command — three deterministic calls, no negotiation between tools.

example.code: $ black src/users.py $ isort src/users.py $ flake8 src/users.py # disagrees with black on line length $ ruff check src/users.py # also installed
example.note: Four tools, two disagreements; the agent rewrites the same line three times across reflexion ticks.

antipattern.description: Keeping ESLint installed "for the team's existing rules" while adding biome on the side.
antipattern.reason: The two formatters fight on quotes, semicolons, and trailing commas; the inner loop becomes "biome fixes, eslint un-fixes, repeat" and tests never get to run.

section.reference: Biome documentation — single toolchain for the web.
